key_note: None of these rules carry a -k key. They still emit events and MUST be tiered. The missing key is an Option-2 'unattributable / low-value' signal (f3 section 7 item 2), not a parse error. key=null on every rule; --from-log per-key aggregation cannot attribute their volume (it lands in an unkeyed bucket).

rules.note: unrestricted execve HIGH, but UNATTRIBUTABLE (no key). 1200 B/event.
single-file write/attr watch, no key. 900 B/event (file-watch).
narrowed openat MEDIUM (~0.3x unrestricted), no key. 1200 B/event.

rate_band_basis: f3 section 6: execve HIGH; single-file watch like #11 write band; narrowed openat ~0.3x. Mixed byte shapes (1200 syscall, 900 watch).
